entry.content:
I linked to a terminal music player over at the hub this morning, which I’ve been playing with since then. I say Wow!

It’s got an excellent equalizer and visualizer. You can play any radio stream or pick one from Radio Browser, which I’m familiar with from using Home Assistant. Or play something from your own music library. I discovered a new-to-me Blues stream searching Radio Browser via the player. I like that.

Things like this make me appreciate how I spend my time... and appreciate some people I’m blessed to know. Thanks again mikael!
entry.summary: I linked to a terminal music player over at the hub this morning, which I’ve been playing with since then. I say Wow! It’s got an excellent equalizer and visualizer. You can play any radio stream or pick one from Radio Browser, which I’m familiar with from using Home Assistant. Or play something from your own music library. I discovered a new-to-me Blues stream searching Radio Browser via the player. I like that. Things like this make me appreciate how I spend my time... and appreciate some people I’m blessed to know. Thanks again mikael!

entry.title: M.A.Q.A.
entry.content:
Re-labeled Elmer's Washable School Glue Stick
Package © 2025 Newell Office Brands.
entry.summary: Re-labeled Elmer's Washable School Glue Stick Package © 2025 Newell Office Brands.

entry.title: bad Apple…bad!
entry.content:
03/25/26-Update: It was nice while it lasted. Oh well…

01/30/26-Update: Problem resolved. If you’ve experienced similar issues, get in touch and I’ll point you to the solution I found. Thx.

I find it hard to believe that Apple is programmatically nagging users to update to Tahoe (despite compatibility issues) without giving users the choice to say no.

Some time ago, I learned the hard way that Tahoe won’t even boot on my 2020 iMac (Intel). And I can’t get back the huge amount of time consumed in recovering my machine. Back then, I chose to do the update, but currently, many users with Automatic Updates turned on are learning the same lesson.

Why doesn’t Apple know about these compatibility issues? And if they do, why don’t they care about wasting their customers’ time?

What in hell happened to them?

Absolutely incredible!

(edited 01/25/26)
entry.summary: 03/25/26-Update: It was nice while it lasted. Oh well… 01/30/26-Update: Problem resolved. If you’ve experienced similar issues, get in touch and I’ll point you to the solution I found. Thx. I find it hard to believe that Apple is programmatically nagging users to update to Tahoe (despite compatibility issues) without giving users the choice to say no. Some time ago, I learned the hard way that Tahoe won’t even boot on my 2020 iMac (Intel). And I can’t get back the huge amount of time consumed in recovering my machine. Back then, I chose to do the update, but currently, many users with Automatic Updates turned on are learning the same lesson. Why doesn’t Apple know about these compatibility issues? And if they do, why don’t they care about wasting their customers’ time? What in hell happened to them? Absolutely incredible! (edited 01/25/26)

entry.content:
turmoil created
chaos seems the wrong path to choose
for knowing darkness

#haiku
entry.summary: turmoil created chaos seems the wrong path to choose for knowing darkness #haiku

entry.title: livehaiku 08-21-25
entry.content:
maybe lost someone
a looming dark memory
overwhelming blues

a grand play-to-pay
happening in front of us
a train wreck looming

lost?

motherf*in’ blues
chew you up and spit you out
leave you but to chill

just chill!

reload
entry.summary: maybe lost someone a looming dark memory overwhelming blues a grand play-to-pay happening in front of us a train wreck looming lost? motherf*in’ blues chew you up and spit you out leave you but to chill just chill! reload

entry.title: critters out back
entry.content: Photo by Kim

entry.title: sometimes you put it out there…

link.title: sometimes you put it out there…

entry.content:
…and sometimes you should take it back.

That feeling when I publicly put something out there and come to realize it could be taken as something entirely different than what I intended to say.

A downside of being a sarcastic wiseguy (like myself) is it’s easy to lose sight of the fact that other sarcastic wiseguys are probably the only ones who will get the joke… #

I’m thankful a part of my routine is to read everything I write a second time after at least a few hours. Everything I do is a work in progress and mistakes are just another part of the routine.

Now you know why I feel free to edit what I write. Writing like coding, always has room to improve.

Cheers,
joe
entry.summary: …and sometimes you should take it back. That feeling when I publicly put something out there and come to realize it could be taken as something entirely different than what I intended to say. A downside of being a sarcastic wiseguy (like myself) is it’s easy to lose sight of the fact that other sarcastic wiseguys are probably the only ones who will get the joke… # I’m thankful a part of my routine is to read everything I write a second time after at least a few hours. Everything I do is a work in progress and mistakes are just another part of the routine. Now you know why I feel free to edit what I write. Writing like coding, always has room to improve. Cheers, joe

entry.title: good morning from Li’l Joe!
a preamble of sorts-2
entry.content:
in a longform life
so many damn things to say
when haiku falls short…
entry.summary: in a longform lifeso many damn things to saywhen haiku falls short…

entry.title: talking to myself
entry.content:
A parody if you will:

Talking to myself (ah, oh, oh-oh)
Talking to myself (ah, oh, oh-oh)
When there's nothing to lose and there's nothing to prove
Well, I'm talking to myself, ah, oh, oh-oh

Sorry Billy!

My whacked creativity tends to talk to me directly at times. I give myself a lot of advice, sometimes in the form of words, other times in the form of music. I guess I’m a bit narcissistic that way. Like the way I’m linking to myself here - eeeew!

Okay Joe, let’s stop this right now!

Carry on...
entry.summary: A parody if you will: Talking to myself (ah, oh, oh-oh) Talking to myself (ah, oh, oh-oh) When there's nothing to lose and there's nothing to prove Well, I'm talking to myself, ah, oh, oh-oh Sorry Billy! My whacked creativity tends to talk to me directly at times. I give myself a lot of advice, sometimes in the form of words, other times in the form of music. I guess I’m a bit narcissistic that way. Like the way I’m linking to myself here - eeeew! Okay Joe, let’s stop this right now! Carry on...
